title: Cleidiocarpon cavaleriei
intro: Cleidiocarpon cavaleriei, also known as Cavaleriea, is a plant species within the family Apocynaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, and it is traditionally used to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Cleidiocarpon cavaleriei include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, the plant is commonly used to prepare herbal infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions.
taxonomy: Cleidiocarpon cavaleriei is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is native to China and belongs to the order Lamiales. The classification of Cleidiocarpon cavaleriei is as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Lamiales, family Rubiaceae, genus Cleidiocarpon, species C. cavaleriei.
